title: What Economics Actually Is
body:
Economics studies how people and institutions allocate scarce resources. That means it is really about choices: what gets produced, who gets it, what it costs, and what happens when incentives change.

Microeconomics looks at households, firms, and individual markets. Macroeconomics zooms out to inflation, unemployment, growth, interest rates, and the policy levers governments use to steer the economy.

The field is useful because it turns vague arguments about fairness or efficiency into explicit trade-offs that can be examined, measured, and challenged.

The best economics keeps the human messiness in view instead of pretending people are perfectly rational calculators.
keyPoints: Economics is model-based but grounded in data
furtherReading: Principles of Economics by Gregory Mankiw
Economics in One Lesson by Henry Hazlitt
The Undercover Economist by Tim Harford
